_meta.note: the RETURN half of exam distribution; data lands in evaluation_import_batches (OMR import)

title: School answer-sheet upload — the return half of exam distribution (UI-wired)
business_reason: Completes the distribution loop (FR-MATERIAL-RELEASE-0018 is the send half): after a school prints + administers the exam, it returns the answer sheets. A school-facing upload creates an import batch + ingests the OMR rows (reusing FR-OMR-IMPORT-0010), which then flows into scoring (FR-OMR-SCORING-0008) → results. Wired UI end-to-end per P0.10 (upload control upstream + status list downstream, reachable from nav).

design.route: POST /api/school/answer-sheets — school-scoped; resolves the school's exam cycle (participation→olympiad→exam_cycle, server-side); creates evaluation_import_batches (source_type school_upload, school_id=actor, uploaded_by=SYSTEM_STAFF_ID since uploaded_by is a staff FK — created ON the school's behalf, real actor in the audit); then ingestOmrBatch persists the responses. GET lists the school's own answer-sheet batches.
design.ui: Custom page /school/answer-sheets — UPSTREAM upload control + DOWNSTREAM list (batch code · status · valid/invalid counts). Nav link added via gen_ui SCHOOL_PLACEHOLDERS + a SCHOOL_CUSTOM_PAGES skip-set so the custom page isn't clobbered by a generated stub.
design.why_custom_not_generated: evaluation_import_batches is shared with the STAFF import module + uploaded_by is a NOT-NULL staff FK, so table-keyed createCompute / the generated school create can't set the school-specific values without breaking staff imports — hence a dedicated school route.

param_14.question: How does a school return its administered answer sheets?
param_14.answer: From the school portal Answer Sheets page, the school uploads the OMR CSV. The server creates an import batch for the school's exam cycle (on the school's behalf — uploaded_by is a staff FK, so the system id is used and the real school actor is audited) and ingests the rows (reusing the OMR import). The page then lists each upload's status + valid/invalid counts. Operations scores it from there.
param_14.answer_source: exam_material_ops/answer_sheet_policy.json (school-scoped, candidate_id/school_id required, OMR supported); evaluation_import_batches (source_type school_upload); FR-OMR-IMPORT-0010 ingest
param_14.security_implications: school-scoped (school_id resolved server-side, never from the browser); the exam cycle is resolved from the school's own participation (not client-supplied); scoring/approval stays staff-gated (the school can only upload, not score); the upload is audited with the real school actor.
param_14.approval_implications: the batch lands as validated (parsed) and is handed to operations for scoring/approval — the school does not self-score.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: scoring (FR-OMR-SCORING-0008) consumes the batch; binary raw-scan storage (source_file is uuid) deferred; per-candidate validation against the school roster is a follow-up.
param_14.assumptions: one active exam cycle per school (most-recent chosen if several); OMR CSV format from parseOmrResponses.
